meaning: adj.有力的,巨大的,激烈的,猛烈的,极大的
examples: France and Germany flouted the pact, ignoring threats of swingeing fines.
These powers are neither as new or as swingeing as ministers suggest, however.
Even France made its wealth tax less swingeing; its top income-tax rate is 41%.
The sword ban is less swingeing, since exceptions will be made for martial-arts clubs and collectors.
If the cuts are swingeing, then the prize for inventors everywhere will be reduced.
